area: Data layer: tradingagents/dataflows/ (vendor routing, yfinance + Alpha Vantage providers, stockstats indicators, caching) plus tradingagents/agents/utils/*_tools.py LangChain tool wrappers
summary:
ARCHITECTURE â€” Three layers: (1) LangChain @tool wrappers in C:\Users\randl\Documents\GitHub\TradingAgents\tradingagents\agents\utils\{core_stock_tools,technical_indicators_tools,fundamental_data_tools,news_data_tools}.py â€” each is a thin shim that calls route_to_vendor("<method>", *args). (2) Routing in C:\Users\randl\Documents\GitHub\TradingAgents\tradingagents\dataflows\interface.py. (3) Vendor implementations: y_finance.py / yfinance_news.py / stockstats_utils.py (yfinance, keyless) and alpha_vantage*.py (Alpha Vantage, requires ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY env var).

ROUTING (interface.py): TOOLS_CATEGORIES maps 4 categories to 9 method names: core_stock_apisâ†’[get_stock_data]; technical_indicatorsâ†’[get_indicators]; fundamental_dataâ†’[get_fundamentals, get_balance_sheet, get_cashflow, get_income_statement]; news_dataâ†’[get_news, get_global_news, get_insider_transactions]. VENDOR_LIST = ["yfinance", "alpha_vantage"]. VENDOR_METHODS maps each method to {vendor: impl}. get_vendor(category, method) reads config: tool-level config["tool_vendors"][method] takes precedence over category-level config["data_vendors"][category]. route_to_vendor(method, *args, **kwargs) splits the configured vendor string on commas (so "yfinance,alpha_vantage" is a priority list), appends all remaining vendors as fallbacks, and tries each impl in order. CRITICAL: only AlphaVantageRateLimitError triggers fallback to the next vendor; any other exception propagates (yfinance functions mostly catch their own errors and return error strings, so they rarely raise). If no vendor succeeds: RuntimeError(f"No available vendor for '{method}'").

CONFIG (config.py + default_config.py): module-level _config initialized from tradingagents.default_config.DEFAULT_CONFIG; set_config(dict) merges overrides; get_config() returns a copy. Default DEFAULT_CONFIG["data_vendors"] = {"core_stock_apis": "yfinance", "technical_indicators": "yfinance", "fundamental_data": "yfinance", "news_data": "yfinance"} and "tool_vendors": {} â€” i.e., THE DEFAULT IS 100% YFINANCE / KEYLESS. "data_cache_dir" defaults to ~/.tradingagents/cache (env override TRADINGAGENTS_CACHE_DIR).

YFINANCE FUNCTIONS (all keyless, unofficial Yahoo endpoints; wrapped in yf_retry from stockstats_utils.py â€” on yfinance.exceptions.YFRateLimitError (HTTP 429) retries up to max_retries=3 with exponential backoff base_delay=2.0 â†’ sleeps 2s, 4s, 8s, then re-raises):
1. get_YFin_data_online(symbol, start_date, end_date) [y_finance.py]: yf.Ticker(symbol.upper()).history(start, end); strips tz from index, rounds Open/High/Low/Close/Adj Close to 2dp; returns CSV string prefixed with header lines "# Stock data for {SYM} from {start} to {end}", "# Total records: N", "# Data retrieved on: ...". Empty â†’ "No data found for symbol ..." string.
2. get_stock_stats_indicators_window(symbol, indicator, curr_date, look_back_days) [y_finance.py]: computes indicators LOCALLY with the stockstats library over cached OHLCV â€” no per-indicator network call. Supported indicators (best_ind_params keys): close_50_sma, close_200_sma, close_10_ema, macd, macds, macdh, rsi, boll, boll_ub, boll_lb, atr, vwma, mfi. Unsupported â†’ raises ValueError listing valid names. Bulk path _get_stock_stats_bulk: load_ohlcv(symbol, curr_date) â†’ stockstats.wrap(df) â†’ df[indicator] triggers calc â†’ dict dateâ†’value. Output: "## {indicator} values from {before} to {curr_date}:\n\n" + one "YYYY-MM-DD: value" line per calendar day (non-trading days = "N/A: Not a trading day (weekend or holiday)") + blank lines + canned indicator description text. On bulk failure falls back to per-day StockstatsUtils.get_stock_stats.
   load_ohlcv(symbol, curr_date) [stockstats_utils.py]: validates symbol via safe_ticker_component (regex ^[A-Za-z0-9._\-\^]+$, max 32 chars, not all dots â€” rejects path traversal); downloads yf.download(symbol, start=today-5y, end=today, multi_level_index=False, progress=False, auto_adjust=True) and caches CSV at {data_cache_dir}/{symbol}-YFin-data-{start}-{end}.csv (filename embeds today's date, so cache naturally expires daily). NOTE: docstring says "15 years" but code uses pd.DateOffset(years=5). Rows after curr_date are filtered out to prevent look-ahead bias. _clean_dataframe coerces dates/numerics, drops NaN Close, ffill/bfill price gaps.
3. get_fundamentals(ticker, curr_date=None) [y_finance.py]: yf.Ticker(ticker).info; formats ~28 labeled fields (Name, Sector, Market Cap, PE Ratio (TTM), Forward PE, PEG, P/B, EPS, Dividend Yield, Beta, 52wk high/low, margins, ROE/ROA, Debt to Equity, Free Cash Flow, etc.), skipping None values; returns "# Company Fundamentals for {SYM}" header + lines. curr_date is IGNORED (look-ahead leak: .info is always current snapshot).
4. get_balance_sheet / get_cashflow / get_income_statement(ticker, freq="quarterly", curr_date=None) [y_finance.py]: yf.Ticker properties quarterly_balance_sheet|balance_sheet, quarterly_cashflow|cashflow, quarterly_income_stmt|income_stmt; filter_financials_by_date drops fiscal-period columns dated after curr_date (look-ahead guard); returns CSV string with "# {Statement} data for {SYM} ({freq})" header. All exceptions caught â†’ "Error retrieving ... : {e}" string.
5. get_insider_transactions(ticker) [y_finance.py]: yf.Ticker.insider_transactions â†’ CSV string with header; no date filtering.
6. get_news_yfinance(ticker, start_date, end_date) [yfinance_news.py]: yf.Ticker(ticker).get_news(count=20); handles nested article["content"] schema (title, summary, provider.displayName, canonicalUrl/clickThroughUrl, pubDate ISO); filters by pub_date in [start_dt, end_dt+1day]; returns "## {ticker} News, from {start} to {end}:" + "### {title} (source: {publisher})" blocks with summary and Link. No results â†’ "No news found for ..." string; exceptions â†’ error string.
7. get_global_news_yfinance(curr_date, look_back_days=7, limit=10) [yfinance_news.py]: runs yf.Search(query=q, news_count=limit, enable_fuzzy_query=True) over 4 hardcoded queries ["stock market economy", "Federal Reserve interest rates", "inflation economic outlook", "global markets trading"], dedupes by title, stops once limit reached, skips articles published more than 1 day after curr_date (look-ahead guard), returns "## Global Market News, from {start} to {curr_date}:" + article blocks.

ALPHA VANTAGE FUNCTIONS (all require ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY; alpha_vantage_common.py get_api_key() raises ValueError if unset â€” and ValueError does NOT trigger vendor fallback in route_to_vendor, so misconfiguring AV as primary without a key hard-fails). _make_api_request(function_name, params) GETs https://www.alphavantage.co/query with function, apikey, source="trading_agents", optional entitlement (from params or module global _current_entitlement); returns raw response TEXT; if the JSON body has an "Information" key containing "rate limit" or "api key" it raises AlphaVantageRateLimitError (this is the only fallback trigger). Free tier is ~25 requests/day. Endpoints: get_stock â†’ TIME_SERIES_DAILY_ADJUSTED (csv, outputsize compact if start <100 days ago else full) filtered client-side by _filter_csv_by_date_range; get_indicator â†’ SMA/EMA/MACD/RSI/BBANDS/ATR endpoints (csv), parses 'time' column plus mapped value column (col_name_map: macdâ†’MACD, macdsâ†’MACD_Signal, macdhâ†’MACD_Hist, bollâ†’Real Middle Band, boll_ubâ†’Real Upper Band, boll_lbâ†’Real Lower Band, rsiâ†’RSI, atrâ†’ATR, close_10_emaâ†’EMA, close_50_sma/close_200_smaâ†’SMA), emits same "## {INDICATOR} values from ... to ...:" format; vwma is NOT available (returns explanatory message) and mfi is NOT in supported_indicators (raises ValueError); get_fundamentals â†’ OVERVIEW (raw JSON text); get_balance_sheet/get_cashflow/get_income_statement â†’ BALANCE_SHEET/CASH_FLOW/INCOME_STATEMENT, then _filter_reports_by_date which only filters if the result is a dict â€” but _make_api_request returns a STRING, so the look-ahead filter is effectively a NO-OP for AV fundamentals (latent bug); get_news â†’ NEWS_SENTIMENT with tickers + time_from/time_to in YYYYMMDDTHHMM (format_datetime_for_api); get_global_news(curr_date, look_back_days=7, limit=50) â†’ NEWS_SENTIMENT with topics="financial_markets,economy_macro,economy_monetary"; get_insider_transactions â†’ INSIDER_TRANSACTIONS.

TOOL WRAPPERS (LangChain @tool, all return str): get_stock_data(symbol, start_date, end_date); get_indicators(symbol, indicator, curr_date, look_back_days=30) â€” splits comma-separated indicator lists ("LLMs sometimes pass multiple indicators"), calls route_to_vendor per indicator, catches ValueError per item and appends str(e), joins results with "\n\n"; get_fundamentals(ticker, curr_date); get_balance_sheet/get_cashflow/get_income_statement(ticker, freq="quarterly", curr_date=None); get_news(ticker, start_date, end_date); get_global_news(curr_date, look_back_days=7, limit=5) â€” note wrapper default limit=5 vs yfinance impl default 10 and AV default 50; get_insider_transactions(ticker).

TOOLâ†’AGENT BINDING (graph/setup.py, all four analysts use quick_thinking_llm via llm.bind_tools(tools)): market_analyst â†’ [get_stock_data, get_indicators]; social_media_analyst â†’ [get_news]; news_analyst â†’ [get_news, get_global_news]; fundamentals_analyst â†’ [get_fundamentals, get_balance_sheet, get_cashflow, get_income_statement]. get_insider_transactions is imported by fundamentals_analyst.py but NOT in its bound tools list â€” it is currently reachable only via direct route_to_vendor calls.

MINIMAL KEYLESS SET (covers all four categories with default config, zero API keys): market data = get_YFin_data_online; technical indicators = get_stock_stats_indicators_window (stockstats computed locally over cached yf.download OHLCV â€” 13 indicators incl. mfi which AV lacks); company news = get_news_yfinance; macro news = get_global_news_yfinance; fundamentals = yfinance get_fundamentals + get_balance_sheet + get_cashflow + get_income_statement; insider data = yfinance get_insider_transactions. Every Alpha Vantage function is strictly optional (it is only a fallback/alternative vendor, and the default data_vendors config never selects it).

key_components.role: Vendor router: TOOLS_CATEGORIES (4 categories, 9 methods), VENDOR_METHODS map, get_vendor() (tool_vendors overrides data_vendors), route_to_vendor() with comma-separated priority list + fallback chain triggered only by AlphaVantageRateLimitError
Module-global config singleton: initialize_config/set_config/get_config over DEFAULT_CONFIG copy
DEFAULT_CONFIG: data_vendors all 'yfinance' by default, tool_vendors {}, data_cache_dir ~/.tradingagents/cache (TRADINGAGENTS_CACHE_DIR env override)

Keyless yfinance vendor: get_YFin_data_online (OHLCV CSV), get_stock_stats_indicators_window + _get_stock_stats_bulk (13 local stockstats indicators with canned descriptions), get_fundamentals (.info snapshot, 28 fields), get_balance_sheet/get_cashflow/get_income_statement (quarterly/annual, look-ahead filtered), get_insider_transactions
Keyless news: get_news_yfinance (Ticker.get_news(count=20), date-filtered) and get_global_news_yfinance (yf.Search over 4 hardcoded macro queries, dedup by title, look-ahead guard)

yf_retry (3 retries, 2s/4s/8s backoff on YFRateLimitError), load_ohlcv (5y yf.download cached as {symbol}-YFin-data-{start}-{end}.csv, curr_date cutoff vs look-ahead), _clean_dataframe, filter_financials_by_date, StockstatsUtils.get_stock_stats single-date fallback
safe_ticker_component path-injection guard (regex ^[A-Za-z0-9._\-\^]+$, <=32 chars, not all dots); misc date helpers

AV plumbing: get_api_key (ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY, ValueError if unset), _make_api_request (GET alphavantage.co/query, source=trading_agents, entitlement passthrough, raises AlphaVantageRateLimitError on 'Information' rate-limit/api-key messages), _filter_csv_by_date_range, format_datetime_for_api (YYYYMMDDTHHMM)
get_stock: TIME_SERIES_DAILY_ADJUSTED csv, compact/full outputsize by 100-day window, client-side date filter
get_indicator: maps 12 indicator names to SMA/EMA/MACD/RSI/BBANDS/ATR endpoints; vwma unsupported (stub message), no mfi; col_name_map for CSV column extraction; same output format as yfinance path
OVERVIEW/BALANCE_SHEET/CASH_FLOW/INCOME_STATEMENT; _filter_reports_by_date look-ahead filter is a no-op because _make_api_request returns str not dict
@tool get_indicators(symbol, indicator, curr_date, look_back_days=30); splits comma-separated indicator strings, catches per-indicator ValueError

llm_touchpoints: The dataflows package itself makes ZERO LLM calls â€” it is pure HTTP/pandas/stockstats. All return values are plain strings (CSV or markdown-ish text) designed to be dumped into LLM context as ToolMessage content.

Tool binding: graph/setup.py binds the data tools to the quick-think tier via llm.bind_tools(tools) inside each analyst factory: create_market_analyst(quick_thinking_llm) binds [get_stock_data, get_indicators]; create_social_media_analyst(quick_thinking_llm) binds [get_news]; create_news_analyst(quick_thinking_llm) binds [get_news, get_global_news]; create_fundamentals_analyst(quick_thinking_llm) binds [get_fundamentals, get_balance_sheet, get_cashflow, get_income_statement]. Output is free-text reports (analysts are not structured-output at the tool-loop stage); each analyst system prompt ends with an instruction to 'append a Markdown table at the end of the report'.

Indicator descriptions double as prompt content: get_stock_stats_indicators_window / alpha_vantage get_indicator append a canned usage/tips paragraph per indicator (best_ind_params / indicator_descriptions dicts) to the returned tool string, steering the LLM's interpretation.
external_dependencies: yfinance (pip package, KEYLESS, REQUIRED for default config): unofficial Yahoo Finance scraping â€” Ticker.history, yf.download, Ticker.info, quarterly_/balance_sheet, quarterly_/cashflow, quarterly_/income_stmt, insider_transactions, Ticker.get_news, yf.Search. No key, no documented quota, but throttled via HTTP 429 â†’ YFRateLimitError, handled by yf_retry (3 retries, 2s/4s/8s exponential backoff).

stockstats (pip package, LOCAL computation, REQUIRED for default technical indicators): wrap(df) + df[indicator] computes close_50_sma, close_200_sma, close_10_ema, macd, macds, macdh, rsi, boll, boll_ub, boll_lb, atr, vwma, mfi from cached OHLCV. No network.

Alpha Vantage HTTP API (OPTIONAL, fallback vendor only): requires ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY env var; free tier ~25 req/day; rate-limit detected via 'Information' field in JSON body containing 'rate limit' or 'api key' â†’ AlphaVantageRateLimitError â†’ route_to_vendor falls through to yfinance. Endpoints used: TIME_SERIES_DAILY_ADJUSTED, SMA, EMA, MACD, RSI, BBANDS, ATR, OVERVIEW, BALANCE_SHEET, CASH_FLOW, INCOME_STATEMENT, NEWS_SENTIMENT, INSIDER_TRANSACTIONS. Note TIME_SERIES_DAILY_ADJUSTED is a premium endpoint on current AV plans, another reason to treat AV as optional.
Local filesystem cache: data_cache_dir (default ~/.tradingagents/cache) for one OHLCV CSV per symbol per day; required directory but no service.
No OpenAI/Google/Reddit/Finnhub/SimFin dependencies remain in this data layer (older versions had them; current source is strictly yfinance + alpha_vantage).
port_notes: PRESERVE: (1) The 9-method surface and exact signatures â€” get_stock_data(symbol, start_date, end_date); get_indicators(symbol, indicator, curr_date, look_back_days=30); get_fundamentals(ticker, curr_date); get_balance_sheet/get_cashflow/get_income_statement(ticker, freq='quarterly', curr_date=None); get_news(ticker, start_date, end_date); get_global_news(curr_date, look_back_days=7, limit=5); get_insider_transactions(ticker) â€” these names/args are what the agent prompts reference (e.g. news analyst prompt literally cites 'get_news(query, start_date, end_date)' and 'get_global_news(curr_date, look_back_days, limit)'). (2) String-only returns with the exact header formats ('## {indicator} values from X to Y:', '# Stock data for SYM ...', '### {title} (source: {publisher})') since downstream report prompts consume them as text. (3) Look-ahead-bias guards: load_ohlcv cutoff at curr_date, filter_financials_by_date on statement columns, news pub_date <= curr_date+1d â€” essential for backtest validity. (4) The 13-indicator local stockstats set including the per-indicator description paragraphs appended to tool output. (5) yf_retry semantics (3 retries, exponential 2s/4s/8s on 429) and the per-symbol daily OHLCV CSV cache keyed by safe_ticker_component-validated symbol. (6) safe_ticker_component validation â€” tool args come from the LLM and are path-interpolated. SIMPLIFY/REPLACE: drop Alpha Vantage entirely (it is optional, key-gated, ~25 req/day, its TIME_SERIES_DAILY_ADJUSTED is premium, and its look-ahead filter for fundamentals is a no-op bug anyway); with it gone, route_to_vendor/VENDOR_METHODS/data_vendors/tool_vendors collapse to direct function calls â€” keep a thin registry only if you want future vendor pluggability. The yfinance-only set fully covers all four categories keyless: get_YFin_data_online (market), get_stock_stats_indicators_window (indicators, computed locally), get_news_yfinance + get_global_news_yfinance (news), yfinance get_fundamentals/get_balance_sheet/get_cashflow/get_income_statement/get_insider_transactions (fundamentals + insider). For the Claude Agent SDK port, re-expose these as SDK @tool definitions (the LangChain @tool decorator and route_to_vendor are the only framework couplings; bodies are framework-free) and consider also binding get_insider_transactions to the fundamentals agent â€” current code imports it there but omits it from the bound tools list. KNOWN QUIRKS TO DECIDE ON: load_ohlcv docstring claims 15y but fetches 5y; yfinance get_fundamentals ignores curr_date (current-snapshot leak in backtests); yf.Ticker.get_news only returns ~recent articles (count=20) so historical backtest dates often yield 'No news found'; global news queries are 4 hardcoded English strings; wrapper default limit=5 overrides impl defaults (10 yfinance / 50 AV). No LLM keys are needed anywhere in this subsystem â€” it is fully operable under subscription-auth Claude with zero paid API keys.
